seo.title: Building Agentic RAG with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build agentic rag with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Llama Stack provides a standardized Python interface for building sophisticated Llama-powered agents with built-in safety, memory, and tool capabilities, making it ideal for Agentic RAG systems that autonomously plan queries and synthesize multi-source information. Guardrails are essential due to the high-risk nature of autonomous retrieval agents, where failures in source faithfulness, hallucination, or query planning can propagate misinformation. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs through post-processing verification of agent turns.

architectureOverview.heading: Agentic RAG Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
Agentic RAG with Llama Stack follows a multi-turn agent loop where the PlanningAgent generates query strategies, RetrievalAgent executes vector and keyword searches across multiple sources (databases, web, enterprise docs), and SynthesisAgent generates responses with source attribution. Llama Stack's SafetyShield filters inputs/outputs, MemoryBank maintains conversation context and retrieved chunks, and ToolAgent handles external API calls for real-time data. The architecture emphasizes autonomous loop control with turn limits and structured tool responses.

Key components include the LlamaStackClient for agent orchestration, custom tools for retrieval (Pinecone, Weaviate, etc.), and memory-backed reasoning traces. The agent iteratively refines queries based on retrieval quality scores and synthesizes final answers with verifiable citations. This creates a robust but complex system prone to drift without verification.

Vex wraps the final agent response before delivery, verifying source faithfulness and hallucination using the high-risk profile configuration.

risks.name: Safety shield false positives
risks.description: Llama Stack's SafetyShield blocks benign technical content about security or medical topics
risks.mitigation: Vex custom safety assertions override with domain-specific allowlists

risks.name: Agent loop exhaustion
risks.description: Turn limits exceeded without resolution due to poor query planning
risks.mitigation: Vex query planning accuracy checks terminate ineffective loops early

risks.name: Tool schema violations
risks.description: Retrieval tools return malformed JSON breaking synthesis
risks.mitigation: Vex structured output enforcement on tool responses

risks.name: Memory hallucination
risks.description: MemoryBank associates wrong documents to conversation context
risks.mitigation: Vex source faithfulness verification traces memory retrievals

implementation.title: Initialize Llama Stack Client
implementation.description: Set up the core LlamaStackClient with agent configuration for Agentic RAG
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStackClient
from llama_stack_client.types import AgentConfig

client = LlamaStackClient(
    model='llama-3.2-90b',
    base_url='https://api.llamastack.ai/v1',
    api_key='your-api-key'
)

rag_agent_config = AgentConfig(
    name='agentic-rag',
    system_prompt='You are an Agentic RAG expert. Plan queries, retrieve from multiple sources, synthesize with citations.',
    max_turns=8,
    tools=['retrieval_tool', 'web_search_tool'],
    memory=True
)

rag_agent = client.create_agent(config=rag_agent_config)

implementation.title: Multi-Source Retrieval Tool
implementation.description: Custom tool implementation for Pinecone vector search and keyword fallback

implementation.title: Agent Loop Execution
implementation.description: Execute the full Agentic RAG workflow with memory and safety
implementation.code:
async def execute_agentic_rag(query: str) -> str:
    conversation = client.new_conversation()
    
    # Initial planning turn
    planning_turn = await rag_agent.send_message(
        conversation_id=conversation.id,
        message=query,
        safety_check=True
    )
    
    # Autonomous loop
    for turn in range(rag_agent.config.max_turns):
        response = await rag_agent.process_turn(conversation_id=conversation.id)
        if response.is_final:
            return response.content
    
    return 'Max turns exceeded'

implementation.title: Citation Synthesis
implementation.description: Final synthesis step with source attribution formatting
implementation.code:
def format_with_citations(synthesis: str, sources: List[Dict]) -> str:
    formatted = synthesis
    for i, source in enumerate(sources, 1):
        formatted += f'\n\n[{i}] {source["title"]} (Source ID: {source["id"]})'
        formatted += f'\nRelevance: {source["score"]:.2f}'
    return formatted

# In agent response handler
final_response = format_with_citations(
    response.synthesis,
    response.retrieved_sources
)

vexIntegration.title: Basic Vex Verification Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Llama Stack agent responses with Vex high-risk verification
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

@vex.workflow(
    risk_profile='high',
    assertions=[
        'source_faithfulness',
        'no_hallucination',
        'structured_citations',
        'query_relevance'
    ]
)
async def verified_agentic_rag(query: str) -> str:
    raw_response = await execute_agentic_rag(query)
    
    verified = vex.verify(
        raw_response,
        context={
            'retrieved_sources': conversation.sources,
            'query_plan': planning_turn.content,
            'agent_turns': conversation.turn_history
        }
    )
    
    return verified.content if verified.passed else verified.failure_explanation

vexIntegration.title: Source Faithfulness Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify synthesis matches retrieved chunks with citation validation

vexIntegration.title: Query Planning Validation
vexIntegration.description: Ensure agent planning turns produce relevant retrieval strategies
vexIntegration.code:
@vex.assertion('query_planning_accuracy')
def validate_query_plan(plan: str, original_query: str) -> bool:
    plan_embedding = embed(plan)
    query_embedding = embed(original_query)
    
    # Check if plan addresses core query intent
    intent_match = cosine_similarity(plan_embedding, query_embedding) > 0.75
    
    # Check for retrieval strategy presence
    has_strategy = any(keyword in plan.lower() for keyword in 
                      ['retrieve', 'search', 'vector', 'keyword', 'sources'])
    
    return intent_match and has_strategy

# Called within Vex verification
planning_verified = validate_query_plan(planning_turn.content, user_query)
